plugins.name: Overture
plugins.tagline: The 808 boost — tighten your low end before the gain hits.
plugins.description: A TS-808-style tight overdrive/boost for the pre-amp tightening stage metal guitarists run in front of a high-gain amp: it strips low end before the clipper so palm mutes stay tight instead of farting out into the gain stage, then drives an oversampled, selectable-voicing clipper for the actual overdrive character.
features: Tight — high-pass pre-emphasis, 20–400 Hz, removes low end before the clipper
Drive — 0–40 dB of gain into the clipper
Voicing — Asymmetric (the original "808 boost" character), Soft Symmetric, or Hard Clip, run inside oversampling to keep aliasing out
Tone — post-clip 4th-order low-pass, 1–8 kHz, for fizz control without touching the fundamental
Level & Mix — output trim and delay-compensated dry/wet; Mix at 0% is a sample-accurate passthrough
Bypass — host-visible soft bypass that crossfades instead of clicking
Oversampling — selectable 2× / 4× / 8×
Full state save/recall

plugins.name: Tenebrae
plugins.tagline: A liturgy of shadows — cascaded high-gain distortion for the heaviest rhythm tone.
plugins.description: A high-gain rhythm-guitar distortion aimed squarely at the core "chug" tone of symphonic metal: a tightening high-pass ahead of three cascaded, oversampled waveshaper stages — each progressively tighter and darker — followed by a passive-style 3-band tone stack. Pair it with a cabinet IR loader (such as Nave) for the final voicing.
features: Tight — high-pass pre-emphasis, 20–300 Hz, so palm mutes stay percussive
Gain — 0–40 dB into the 3-stage waveshaper cascade
3-stage cascade — progressively tighter, more asymmetric stages inside 8× oversampling
Voicing — Tight / Loose switch between two complete cascade voicings
Bright — fixed pre-cascade high-shelf switch, modelled on an amp channel's bright switch
Bass / Mid / Treble — passive-style tone stack, ±15 dB per band
Tone Voice — Flat / Scoop / Boost one-switch tilt for instant rhythm-tone shapes
Level & Mix — output trim and delay-compensated dry/wet

plugins.name: Nave
plugins.tagline: Where the sound resonates — impulse-response cabinet simulation for guitar and bass DI.
plugins.description: A cabinet impulse-response loader for reamping guitar and bass DI tracks: load a cab (or full-rig) IR and Nave convolves it with your signal through a zero-latency partitioned convolution engine, then shapes the result with a simulated mic-distance control, post-convolution filters, dry/wet mix, and output trim. With no IR loaded it runs a unit impulse — a true passthrough out of the box.
features: Two independent IR slots — load any WAV/AIFF impulse response; loading happens off the audio thread and never blocks playback
IR Blend — crossfade IR A / IR B with automatic inter-IR phase alignment, so blending never comb-filters
Zero-latency convolution — uniformly partitioned engine, no plugin delay compensation overhead
Distance — simulated mic-to-cab distance (proximity-effect bass and air-absorption darkening)
LoCut / HiCut — post-convolution filters (20–800 Hz / 2–20 kHz) with explicit off positions
Mix & Level — dry/wet (default fully wet) and ±24 dB output trim
State recall includes both loaded IR file paths

plugins.name: Silentium
plugins.tagline: Silence between the storms — a tight lookahead noise gate for palm-muted rhythm.
plugins.description: A tight, lookahead noise gate built to kill amp hiss and hum in the silence between palm-muted chugs: it detects transients on a sidechain-filtered copy of the input, opens fast with a lookahead head start so it never clips the leading edge of a pick attack, and uses separate open/close thresholds so a hovering signal can never chatter the gate.
features: Threshold with fixed 3 dB hysteresis — the gate can never chatter on a signal hovering near one value
Lookahead — 0–20 ms head start so the gate never clips a pick attack; reported to the host as latency
Attack / Hold / Release — full envelope control (0.1–50 ms / 0–500 ms / 5–500 ms)
Range — floor attenuation down to −80 dB while closed
SC HPF — sidechain-only high-pass, 20–500 Hz, keeps hum from falsely holding the gate open
Knee — 0–24 dB soft knee, from classic hard snap to smooth gain blend
Duck — inverts the gain computer into a ducker
Listen — audition the sidechain detection signal
External sidechain input — key the gate from another track

plugins.name: Requiem
plugins.tagline: A cathedral in a box — cinematic convolution reverb for orchestral and choral space.
plugins.description: A cinematic convolution reverb built for orchestral and choral space in symphonic metal productions — cathedral, hall, and chamber tails for strings, choir, and everything that needs to bloom into a wide, dark stereo image behind the wall of guitars. Its impulse response is generated procedurally, off the audio thread — or load your own WAV/AIFF IR to override it entirely.
features: Procedural impulse response — generated from Decay, Damping, Space and Early/Late Balance; no sample library, no licensing
Space — Cathedral / Hall / Chamber early-reflection density and spread
Freeze — sustains the tail's spectral content as a bounded, convolution-based pad
Modulation — subtle chorus-style movement on the wet tail only; 0% is bit-identical
Pre-Delay — 0–250 ms to keep rhythm parts tight while strings and choir bloom behind them
Width — 0–200% mid/side scaling of the wet stereo image
User IR override — load your own captured space, persisted in the plugin state
Latency-compensated Mix and post-mix Output trim

plugins.name: Seraph
plugins.tagline: Voices from above — a choir and vocal processor for operatic symphonic metal.
plugins.description: A channel-strip-style choir and vocal processor for operatic symphonic-metal vocals: it tames sibilance, adds airy openness above the vocal's natural top end, glues dynamics together with a gentle broadband compressor, and thickens a lead or choir line with a click-free four-voice doubler.
features: De-Ess — zero-latency dynamic EQ with tunable center frequency (3–12 kHz) and a Listen mode for tuning by ear
Air — fixed 12 kHz high-shelf for openness above the presence range
Gentle Compressor — single-knob broadband glue, scaling up to a deliberately gentle 3:1
Doubler — four independently modulated, detuned voices at per-voice pan positions; click-free by design
Double Detune / Width — cents detune and stereo spread of the doubled voices
Mix & Output — overall dry/wet blend and output trim
Zero reported latency — nothing in the chain needs host delay compensation

plugins.name: Aureate
plugins.tagline: Gild the orchestra — tape and console saturation glue for strings, brass and layers.
plugins.description: A tape/console saturation "glue" plugin for orchestral material — strings, brass, and layered tracks that need cohesion and analog warmth without sounding like a guitar pedal. A 4×-oversampled, character-selectable saturator meets tape-transport artefacts and a console-style tilt EQ.
features: Character — Tape / Console / Valve saturator transfer-function families, 4× oversampled
Warmth — saturator asymmetry plus a gentle tape-style pre-clip HF rolloff in one knob
Bias — independent saturator asymmetry trim on top of Warmth
Wow/Flutter — tape-transport speed instability, off by default
Hiss — shaped tape-noise floor mixed into the wet path, off by default
Tone — console-style tilt EQ, plus independent HF/LF trim shelves (±6 dB)
Mix — delay-compensated dry/wet; 0% is a sample-accurate passthrough
Full latency reporting for host plugin-delay compensation

plugins.name: Firmament
plugins.tagline: Open the heavens — a stereo widener and imager for lush symphonic layers.
plugins.description: A Mid/Side stereo widener and imager that gives lush symphonic-metal layers — strings, choirs, synth pads — a wider, more open stereo image without smearing the low end: an optional multiband crossover keeps sub-bass centered regardless of how wide the rest of the spectrum is pushed.
features: Width — 0–200% Mid/Side width scaling; 100% is an unmodified pass-through of the input image
Bass Mono Freq / Low Width — optional crossover (0–500 Hz) that keeps sub-bass centered while the highs go wide
Auto Mono Safety — correlation-driven Side attenuation that reins in heavily out-of-phase sources
Haas Mode — optional 0–40 ms widening for genuinely mono material; a clearly documented trade-off, off by default
Mono-safe by construction — the mono downmix is provably identical to the input's (Haas Mode aside)
Zero latency — TPT Linkwitz-Riley crossover, no oversampling, no lookahead anywhere
Running correlation estimate exposed for a future GUI meter
Output — ±24 dB trim

plugins.name: Triptych
plugins.tagline: Three panels, one altarpiece — a 3-band multiband compressor for dense mixes.
plugins.description: A three-band multiband compressor for dense symphonic-metal mixes: two Linkwitz-Riley crossovers split the signal into Low, Mid and High bands, each with its own full-featured compressor, console-style mute/solo, and an optional brickwall limiter on the high band.
features: Two crossovers — Low/Mid (40 Hz–1 kHz) and Mid/High (400 Hz–12 kHz) with enforced band-order separation under automation
Per-band compression — Threshold, Ratio (up to 20:1), Attack, Release and Makeup for each of Low / Mid / High
Per-band Mute/Solo — console semantics; compressors keep running under solo, so no re-attack pop on unmute
High-band limiter — optional brickwall stage after the High band's compressor
Zero added latency — minimum-phase crossovers and envelopes, no lookahead
Output — master trim, ±24 dB
Full state save/recall

plugins.name: Apotheosis
plugins.tagline: The final ascension — a lookahead true-peak brickwall limiter for the master.
plugins.description: A lookahead brickwall true-peak limiter for the master bus — the final gate before export: input gain drives an oversampled true-peak detector, a lookahead delay makes gain reduction instantaneous rather than reactive, and a smooth release relaxes it back once the peak has passed. The output's true (inter-sample) peak never exceeds the Ceiling.
features: True-peak detection — 4× oversampled, so inter-sample peaks are caught and limited, not just sample-domain peaks
Ceiling — −12 to 0 dBTP target, default −1.0 dBTP; the output never exceeds it
Lookahead — 0.1–20 ms; makes gain-reduction attack instantaneous and click-free
Release — 5–1000 ms, log-mapped, with Exponential / Linear / Smooth curve shapes
Clip Mix — blends the transparent limiter with a tanh soft-clip character, same ceiling guarantee
Dither — Off / 16-bit / 24-bit TPDF at the output word length
Metering — gain reduction, output true peak, and Momentary/Short-Term/Integrated LUFS (engine-side)
Full state save/recall

plugins.name: Crypta
plugins.tagline: Split your bass. Compress the lows. Twist the guts out of the highs.
plugins.description: A Parallax-style bass plugin: it splits your bass into low and high bands with a Linkwitz-Riley crossover, compresses the low band in parallel, and runs the high band through a choice of three distortion voicings before summing everything back through a 4-band EQ and an impulse-response (cab sim) loader.
plugins.note: Formerly Twist Your Guts — renamed as part of the suite's move to Basilica Audio naming. The new plugin identity means DAWs treat it as a new plugin; v0.1.0-era sessions need re-pointing.
features: LR4 crossover band-split — 4th-order Linkwitz-Riley, adjustable 60 Hz–1 kHz
Low band — parallel ("New York style") compressor with makeup gain, wet/dry mix and output level
High band — three distortion voicings, each 4× oversampled: Gnaw (op-amp hard clip), Wool (cascaded soft-clip fuzz), Razor (tight overdrive)
Noise gate — full-band, ahead of the crossover split
4-band EQ — LowShelf / Peak / Peak / HighShelf after the band sum
IR loader — cabinet simulation on the output stage
Delay-compensated signal path — bands time-aligned, oversampling latency reported to the host
Full state save/recall
